meta.title: 樱花学园：心动的十字路口
meta.author: GalGame Generator
meta.description:
刚转学至海边小城樱花学园的内向男生小林悠，在开学首日结识了开朗活泼的班长宫本樱，随后又在图书馆偶遇独自看书的冷淡文学少女雪村千雪，在屋顶天台认识了酷飒成熟的学生会副会长桐岛凛。随着日常相处的深入，悠渐渐发现三位看似光鲜的少女各自藏着不为人知的心事：宫本樱的开朗下是家庭的烦恼，雪村千雪的冷漠源于过去被朋友背叛的创伤，桐岛凛的成熟冷静背后是独自扛下的沉重责任。
悠需要在校园日常的诸多选择中做出决定——和谁共进午餐、放学后陪伴谁、学园祭与谁搭档，这些选择将影响对三位女生的好感度，最终通向不同结局：深入了解角色内心并帮助她们化解困境，可解锁专属治愈好结局；若未认真对待任何一人，则会走向孤独疏离的结局。

commands.text: The golden hues of sunset bleed into the ocean, turning the waves into liquid amber. Seagulls cry softly in the distance, and cherry blossom petals drift on the salty coastal breeze. The shore is quiet save for the crash of waves, a perfect refuge from the chaos of daily life.

else.text: Rin-neechan... you look like you haven’t slept in days. Did something happen with your brother?

then.text: Chiyuki... you asked to come here specifically. Is there something you’ve been wanting to tell me?
Sakura... you’ve been quiet since we left school. Your hands are shaking—are you okay?

else.text: My little brother’s condition got worse. The doctors say he needs a transplant, but the waitlist is long, and the bills are piling up.
I’ve been handling everything—学生会 work, the house, visiting the hospital... I try to be strong, but sometimes it feels like I’m going to collapse. I didn’t want to burden anyone, though.

then.text: You never pushed me to talk about why I keep to myself, did you? Last year, I had a friend I trusted more than anything. We did everything together.
But she spread terrible rumors about me—said I stole her boyfriend. Everyone believed her. I lost all my friends, and since then... I’ve been too scared to let anyone close.
Yu-kun... I’ve been lying to everyone. My parents... they fight every night now. The house is either freezing silent or filled with yelling.
I try to be the cheerful班长 everyone expects at school, but I’m so scared. Scared they’ll split up, and everything I know will break apart.

options.text: ‘我会一直陪着你，我们一起面对’

else.text: I appreciate it. I’ll keep pushing through—for my brother, and for myself. Thanks for listening.

then.text: Thank you. I... I needed to hear that. Maybe things will get better eventually.
Thank you, Yu-kun. It means a lot that you’re here. I’ll try to stay strong for both of us.

else.text: Yu... thanks. I don’t know what to say, but having someone to share this with makes it easier. I guess I don’t have to be strong all the time, huh?

then.text: Yu-kun... thank you. For not leaving me alone with these feelings. I think I can start to trust you now.
Yu-kun...!
Thank you so much... I didn’t think anyone would understand how I feel. Having you by my side makes everything feel less scary.

commands.text: The sun dips lower, painting the sky in deep purples and oranges. The waves continue to crash softly, and for a moment, all is quiet—filled with a fragile sense of hope.

The golden sun dips low below the horizon, painting the sky in streaks of tangerine and lavender. Salty ocean breeze tangles in my hair, carrying the distant crash of waves against jagged rocks. A few stray cherry petals drift on the wind—leftovers from the academy festival garlands. I lean against the weathered stone railing, waiting for the person who texted me to meet here.

else.text: I wait for a long time, but no one comes. The sun sets completely, and the sky turns dark, the only light coming from the distant festival lanterns. I realize that maybe I wasn't ready to be there for someone else yet—that I still have a lot to learn about empathy, friendship, and love.

then.text: I wanted to inform you that my brother is in stable condition. Your assistance earlier was greatly appreciated. The学生会 will note your contribution.
You don't have to be formal with me. I was happy to help. Is there anything else I can do for you or your brother?
No... that's all. But... if you ever need help with anything at school—whether it's club matters or academic support—you can come find me. I'll ensure it's handled promptly.

She nods and turns to leave, her posture as straight and confident as ever. But I catch the faint, genuine smile on her face before she's out of sight. It's a small gesture, but it means more than she could ever know.

Sorry for calling you here last minute. My brother's okay now—they just needed to give him some medicine for his asthma. But I... I needed to talk to someone who didn't see me as just the学生会 vice president.
I'm glad he's safe. You looked so stressed earlier... I wanted to help, even if I didn't know how.

I'm used to handling everything on my own—taking care of my brother, running the学生会, keeping up with school. But when you showed up with the first-aid kit and stayed with him until the nurse came... it made me realize I don't have to be strong all the time. That it's okay to ask for help.
I don't know how to say this properly... but thank you. For being there. For seeing me—the real me, not just the girl in the black blazer.

She reaches out and takes my hand, her fingers trembling slightly. For the first time, I see the girl behind the tough exterior—scared, but trying her best. And I know I want to be the one who stands beside her, always.
I wanted to say thank you. For helping me at the festival. Those people were just being annoying, but it's good that someone was there to stop them.
It was no trouble. I know how important your book collection is to you.
...If you ever want to discuss literature, you can find me in the library's window nook. I don't mind the company.

She turns and walks away, her silver hair flowing in the breeze. But not before glancing back at me with a faint, almost imperceptible smile. It's a small step, but I know it's the start of a quiet, steady friendship.
You came. I wasn't sure if you would... after the chaos at the book stall.
Of course I came. I could tell those people were upsetting you. I know how much your books mean to you.

They were old classmates... people I thought were friends once. When you stepped in to stop them... it reminded me that not everyone is going to hurt me. That there are people who will stand up for me, even when I can't.
I brought this for you. It's my favorite book—well-loved, as you can see. I wrote a note inside... about how you make me feel safe. Like I don't have to hide anymore.

She hands me the tattered hardcover, her fingers brushing mine. For a moment, we stand in silence, watching the sun disappear below the waves. I know this is just the start of something quiet, gentle, and real.
Thanks again for helping with the ingredients earlier! The matcha parfaits sold out in minutes—everyone loved them!
No problem at all! I'm glad it worked out. You did most of the hard work setting up the stall anyway.
Hey, don't sell yourself short! Oh, and if you ever need help with anything—homework, moving stuff, whatever—just holler! I'm here for you.
She waves and turns to run back toward the festival, her pink hair glowing in the sunset. We don't get closer than this, but knowing we're good friends is more than enough for now.
Umm... sorry for making you wait here. I just... wanted to talk somewhere quiet, away from the festival's noisy crowds.
It's totally fine. This place is perfect. Did you want to thank me again for rushing to get the matcha powder earlier?

Well... that's part of it. But mostly... I wanted to tell you that you're the first person who didn't just see my smile and turn away. You noticed I was panicking even before I said anything. It made me feel like I didn't have to pretend to be strong all the time.
From now on, I want to share both the happy and the hard parts with you. If you'll let me?

I pull her into a gentle hug, and she buries her face in my shoulder. The sound of waves mixes with her quiet sobs, and for the first time, I feel like I truly understand what it means to be someone's safe place.

sakura_academy_cafeteria.bgm: bgm/轻松活泼，充满日常感的轻快节奏纯音乐.mp3

commands.text: Chapter 2
commands.subtitle: 午餐的心动选择

commands.text: 刚转学的小林悠端着餐盘站在食堂中央，耳边是同学们喧闹的谈笑声，鼻尖萦绕着咖喱饭和味增汤的香气。窗外飘进来的几片樱花花瓣落在光洁的地板上，和周围的热闹格格不入——他攥紧了餐盘的边缘，陌生的环境让他的心跳不由自主地加快了几分。
突然，一道明亮的声音穿透了嘈杂的人群。宫本樱坐在靠窗的大桌旁，周围围着七八个同班同学，她正笑着给身边的女生递纸巾，眼角的余光扫到了悠，立刻挥起手，脸颊上的梨涡陷得更深了。
小林同学！这里这里！快过来坐呀～我特意给你留了位置呢！
悠的目光不自觉地移向食堂的另一侧。雪村千雪独自坐在最角落的靠窗位置，面前只有一个全麦面包和一瓶绿茶，膝盖上摊着一本卷边的《雪国》。她偶尔咬一口面包，视线便飘向窗外的樱树，指尖无意识地摩挲着书页的边缘，仿佛周遭的一切都与她无关。
这时，他注意到桐岛凛提着一个黑色便当盒，脚步匆匆地走向食堂后门的楼梯。她的眉头微蹙，另一只手夹着一叠厚厚的学生会文件，指尖因为用力而泛白——显然是打算避开喧闹的人群，去天台独自处理工作和午餐。
悠的目光在三个方向之间来回游移。热情的班长宫本樱，安静的文学少女雪村千雪，还有总是独来独往的桐岛学姐。他深吸了一口气，知道自己必须做出选择——这或许是他融入这个新校园的第一步，也是触碰不同心事的起点。

options.text: 加入宫本樱的热闹餐桌

sakura_academy_classroom.bgm: bgm/轻快明亮，带着青涩校园感的纯音乐.mp3

commands.text: Chapter 4
commands.subtitle: 心事的微光

commands.text: The last bell of the afternoon fades into the hum of cherry blossom petals drifting through the open windows. Most students have already bolted for the gates, but the classroom still holds the warm glow of sunlight filtering through pink blossoms. A few scattered pencil cases and half-empty water bottles line the desks, and the bulletin board at the front glows with the streamers and hand-drawn sakura we just finished hanging.
Thanks so much for staying behind to help with the bulletin board, Yu-kun! If it weren't for you, I'd still be here fighting with those stubborn streamers that keep falling down.
Ah, it's nothing really... I didn't have anywhere to rush off to, and the board looks amazing now. Your sakura drawings are so detailed—you must have spent ages on them.

As she turns to zip up her canvas bag, her smile falters—stretching too thin around the edges, like a paper cherry blossom about to tear. Her fingers hover over the pocket for a moment, and I catch a glimpse of a crumpled white letter peeking out before she shoves it deeper inside.
Sakura-san... are you okay? You seem a little distracted all of a sudden.

It's just... my parents have been fighting a lot lately. Over stupid things, like who forgot to buy milk or turn off the lights. But the house feels so tense, like a balloon about to pop. I try to keep smiling at school—everyone expects the class president to be the cheerful one, right? But sometimes... I just get tired of pretending everything's fine.

options.text: ‘我会一直陪着你，我们一起面对’

sakura_academy_festival_venue.bgm: bgm/欢快热烈，充满节日氛围的活泼曲调.mp3

commands.text: Chapter 6
commands.subtitle: 心动的结局

commands.text: The sun dips below the cherry blossom-lined school walls, painting the sky in watercolor hues of orange and pink. Paper lanterns glow warmly along the festival paths, and the air hums with laughter, the sizzle of takoyaki, and the sweet scent of honey taiyaki. I’m finishing up arranging plushie prizes at our class’s ring-toss stall, wiping sweat from my brow as the last of the evening crowd filters in.
Oh no—we’re completely out of red bean paste! The taiyaki griddle’s still hot, and there’s a line of kids waiting... Where am I going to find more at this hour?
Please leave me alone. I don’t care what you think about my book choices.

I glance over to the quiet book stall by the library entrance. Two girls from Chiyuki’s old class are leaning against her table, snickering as they flip through her favorite collection of poetry. Her hands are clenched at her sides, and her eyes glisten with unshed tears behind her glasses.
Shota, just hang on a little longer... *pant* The infirmary’s just around the corner. I should have brought your medicine with me today...

Rin’s little brother Shota is clinging to her shoulder, his face pale with pain. Her usual sharp, confident posture is gone—she’s hunched over, struggling to carry his weight while keeping her pace steady. The scar above her eyebrow is visible as she furrows her brow in concern.

options.text: Drop everything and rush to help Sakura with her dessert stall
Finish arranging the prizes first, then go help Sakura
Run over to defend Chiyuki from her old classmates
Wait until the girls leave, then go check on Chiyuki
Hurry to Rin’s side and help her carry Shota to the infirmary
Finish my task first, then catch up to Rin to see if she needs help

sakura_academy_rooftop_terrace.bgm: bgm/沉稳舒缓，带着淡淡的疲惫与释然感的弦乐纯音乐.mp3

commands.text: 傍晚的樱花学园天台被落日染上了一层暖橙色的光晕，远处的海面在夕阳下泛着细碎的金波。咸湿的海风裹挟着樱花瓣轻轻掠过脸颊，将白日的喧嚣都吹散在了风里。
桐岛凛靠在天台的水泥护栏上，学生会外套随意地搭在臂弯里，领口的领带松垮地垂着。平日里锐利的眼眸此刻蒙上了一层疲惫，眼尾微微泛红，显然是刚哭过不久。
凛学姐...我在学生会办公室门口看到你跑出来，就跟着过来了。你没事吧？
我没事，只是今天的工作有点多，累了而已。
可是你的眼睛...红红的，是不是发生什么事了？
...我弟弟，他上周被查出得了罕见的心脏病，下周就要做手术了。
爸妈已经快被手术费压垮了，学生会的事情又不能不管...我好像，快撑不住了。

options.text: ‘我会一直陪着你，我们一起面对’

else.text: 谢谢你的安慰。我会尽力撑过去的。

then.text: 悠...谢谢你。我一直以为我必须一个人扛下所有，没想到...有人愿意陪我一起走下去。

sakura_academy_library_window_nook.bgm: bgm/舒缓轻柔，略带忧郁的钢琴纯音乐.mp3

commands.text: The afternoon sunlight filters through sheer white curtains, dappling the worn wooden desk with gold. A single cherry blossom petal drifts onto the windowsill, carried by the faint sea breeze. In the cozy nook by the window, Yukimura Chiyuki sits motionless, her gaze fixed on the same page of her hardcover novel for what feels like an eternity.
Chiyuki... you haven't turned a page in ten minutes. Is something wrong?
Her fingers tighten around the book spine until her knuckles turn white. She stares at the floor, shoulders slumping as if carrying an invisible weight.
It's... nothing. Just... remembering something stupid.
You don't have to talk about it if you don't want to. But... I'm here if you need someone to listen. No interruptions, no judgments.
...Why are you being so kind to me? Most people give up after I brush them off once.
Because I know what it's like to feel like you're alone in the world. To think no one will ever understand what you're going through. And... I don't want you to have to carry that by yourself.
Last year... I had a best friend. We did everything together—walked to school, shared lunch, even stayed up late writing silly stories together.
I trusted her with everything... including the stories I wrote in secret. The ones that came from... places I never let anyone see.
But she... she copied them. Posted them online under her name, and told everyone I'd stolen her ideas. When I tried to explain, no one believed me. They all called me a liar. A thief.
A single tear slips down Chiyuki's cheek, landing on the book's pages and leaving a small dark spot. She quickly wipes it away, but her voice cracks as she speaks.
I didn't go to school for a week. After that... I just stopped talking to everyone. It was easier than getting hurt again.

options.text: ‘I'll always be here, Chiyuki. We can face this together.’
‘Don't worry... everything will get better eventually.’

bad_ending_lonely.name: 热闹中的孤独身影
bad_ending_lonely.condition: affection_sakura <5 and affection_chiyuki <5 and affection_rin <5 and empathy_level <3
